name: Empty States
description: What a section or page shows when it has nothing to display yet: a centered stack of a 24px icon, a bold title, a caption, and an optional secondary-button call to action, optionally inside a bordered container.

shared.layout: Centered column flex stack, text-align center. The empty state is placed centered inside whatever section has no content (usually a Primary card, see patterns/cards.json).
shared.sits_in: Typically centered inside a Primary card (white surface, 2px 2px 24px rgba(22,52,98,.08) shadow, 16px radius) that stands in for the page's missing content.
shared.placement: Replaces only the content area. Keep filters, search, and other actions visible above it; a table's header row and toolbar stay put, only the row area becomes the empty state.

icon.note: Phosphor icon. Match the icon to the situation. Use CircleDashed as the neutral fallback when no situation-specific icon fits.

subtitle.note: The caption. This is the only element that survives in the Small size.

cta.note: On by default for No content empty states, but not mandatory. Only add a CTA when there is a clear next step; drop it when there is no single clear next step.

spec.layout: inline-flex, align-items center, gap 6, white-space nowrap
spec.note: iconEnd (e.g. an ArrowRight, 16px) is optional and sits after the label.

container.note: Bordered container wrapping the centered stack, used inside a Primary card to frame the empty state. Add it when the empty state has no parent container of its own or the layout needs a clear boundary; skip it when a parent such as a table, panel, or card already frames the space.

Medium.role: The full model: icon + title + subtitle + optional CTA.
whenToUse: Where there is room to guide the user
When the empty state sits in an area with M-sized elements, such as admin pages (use the Medium CTA)
Medium.example: Stores: shown inside the row area of a real entity table (Values Table's entity mode: 0.5px #cbd1db border, 50px header with 50%-opacity neutrals-50 background), CircleDashed icon, title "There are no stores yet", subtitle "Add content manually, generate it from workflows or create it automatically when testing", CTA "Add Store" (Medium). Only the row area holds the empty state; the table header stays visible.

Small.role: Subtitle only. Drops the icon, title, and CTA, leaving just the 12px caption.
whenToUse: Cramped or dense sections where only the empty message fits, such as a dropdown panel or a tight subsection
When the surrounding area uses small components, such as a config panel (pair with a Small CTA if a CTA is genuinely needed)

scenarios.label: First use / no data yet
scenarios.example: Classes: Stack icon, title "No classes yet", subtitle "The categories your model sorts pages into — you need at least two to train.", CTA "Add Class" (Small, no icon)

scenarios.label: No results
scenarios.example: Workflow node search: FlowArrow icon, title "No Results", subtitle "There are no nodes matching your search.", no CTA

scenarios.label: Canvas empty state
scenarios.example: UI Designer canvas: Layout icon, title "UI Designer", subtitle "Drag components from the sidebar", no CTA, no container. Canvas empty states are centered in the view and do not use a bordered container.

guidelines: Use a centered stack to explain why a section has no content and what action fills it
The same model covers every no-content situation; match the icon and copy to the situation
Replaces only the content area; keep filters, search, and other actions visible above it
CTA is on by default for No content empty states but not mandatory; only add one when there is a clear next step

Container is optional; add it when the empty state has no parent container of its own or the layout needs a clear boundary, skip it when a parent such as a table, panel, or card already frames the space
Canvas empty states are centered in the view and do not use a container
Match the size to the components around the empty state
Medium (full model) where there is room to guide the user; use a Medium CTA in areas with M-sized elements such as admin pages
Small (subtitle only) for limited space such as a dropdown panel or a tight subsection; use a Small CTA when the surrounding area uses small components such as a config panel
Fall back to the CircleDashed icon when no situation-specific icon fits

title: Name the state, don't decorate it
Use sentence case, no ending period, and no more than five words
No-data title: "No {items} yet"
No-results title: "No results"
subtitle: Add new information; never restate the title
Write one sentence, sentence case, with an ending period
Default: describe what fills it, such as "UI Flows you create appear here."
When there is no CTA, explain how to start, such as "Drag components from the sidebar."
When value is not obvious, explain why it matters, such as "Metadata keys make content easier to find."
For no-results states, flip to recovery, such as "No nodes match your search."
button: Use verb-first, sentence case copy with no ending period
Match the toolbar or destination label exactly
Include only one button, and only when there is a clear next step
acrossTheApp: Write both states for anything searchable: no-data is not no-results
Use one name per concept across title, subtitle, and button

themes.dark: not documented; the pattern page keeps example mockups light
